qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Missing authentication on admin API endpoints enables unauthenticated DoS and information disclosure
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: Different root cause and code path: this finding concerns unauthenticated Caddy admin handlers in caddy/admin.go, not Unicode case-folding in splitPos()/splitCgiPath().

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: CRLF injection in HTTP header and cookie processing enables response splitting and XSS
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: Different root cause and code path: this finding concerns CRLF handling in go_write_headers(), go_read_cookies(), and splitRawHeader(), not CGI path splitting or Unicode lowercasing offsets.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Session handler state pollution in worker mode enables multi-tenant session data leakage
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: Different root cause and code path: this finding concerns worker-mode PHP session handler snapshot/restore in frankenphp.c, not SCRIPT_NAME/PATH_INFO confusion in CGI path splitting.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described vulnerable code or root cause. The advisory is about splitPos()/splitCgiPath() computing a .php split byte offset on strings.ToLower(path) and applying it to the original Unicode path, while the qualifying findings cover unrelated admin authorization, CRLF header handling, unsafe deserialization, and worker session handler state.
evidence: Advisory GHSA-g966-83w7-6w38 describes Unicode case-folding byte-length expansion in splitPos() and splitCgiPath() causing SCRIPT_NAME/SCRIPT_FILENAME confusion.

Search of the session artifacts found no qualifying report evidence for splitPos, splitCgiPath, SCRIPT_NAME/SCRIPT_FILENAME confusion, Unicode case-folding expansion, or the advisory's .php path split mechanism.
Each qualifying report names a different vulnerable function, endpoint, or sink from the advisory root cause.
